cases.description: Rounds 24.3 up to the nearest multiple of 5. Source: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/ceiling-math-function-80f95d2f-b499-4eee-9f16-f795a8e306c8 (worked example table)
Significance and mode both omitted -> defaults to nearest integer (significance 1). Source: Microsoft CEILING.MATH docs.
Negative number, mode omitted -> unlike the modeless legacy CEILING function, CEILING.MATH's default for negative numbers rounds TOWARD zero.
cases.expected_note: Microsoft worked example: CEILING.MATH(-8.1,2) = -8 ('rounds -8.1 up (toward 0) to the nearest multiple of 2'). This is the modern, explicit-mode successor to legacy CEILING and was designed to match LibreOffice/ODF's CEILING(...,...,Mode) semantics for the default case.

cases.description: Negative number with a nonzero mode argument -> rounds away from zero instead of the toward-zero default.
cases.expected_note: Microsoft worked example: CEILING.MATH(-5.5,2,-1) = -6 ('rounds -5.5 down (away from 0) ... with a mode of -1'). Mode only affects negative numbers; any nonzero mode reverses the default direction.
